item.title: Samsung reaches last-minute deal to avert strike over AI riches
item.description: Work stoppage had threatened to disrupt Korean economy and global artificial intelligence boom

item.title: Asteroid mining and new billionaires: what’s in SpaceX’s IPO filing
item.description: Elon Musk’s rocket-to-AI conglomerate lays out its planetary ambitions

item.title: Anthropic on track for first profitable quarter
item.description: AI lab set to hit milestone ahead of competitors OpenAI and xAI

item.title: Musk’s SpaceX sets out plans for biggest IPO in history
item.description: Satellite and AI company to list on Nasdaq under symbol SPCX

item.title: FirstFT: Nvidia reports better than expected revenue and forecasts in Q1 earnings
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: OpenAI’s IPO could come as early as this week and Xi warns of ‘the law of the jungle’ to Putin

item.title: SpaceX is all ’trucked up
item.description: But Elon Musk is not giving himself mates’ rates

item.title: Nvidia to return more than $80bn to shareholders as it reaps rewards of AI boom
item.description: World’s most valuable company reported better than expected revenue and forecasts

item.title: US state department watchdog investigates Gaza Humanitarian Foundation
item.description: Oversight body scrutinising how $30mn grant was spent by controversial non-profit that had US and Israeli backing

item.title: M&S boss slams ‘preposterous’ proposal to cap food prices
item.description: Stuart Machin’s comments come as government pushes supermarkets to sign up to voluntary price limits for key items

item.title: Zuckerberg promises no more ‘company-wide’ lay-offs after slashing jobs
item.description: Meta chief seeks to reassure disillusioned employees after culling 8,000 roles

item.title: M&S has won over fashionistas but not yet investors
item.description: Consumers rank it first among its peers in the UK on value and style, but its shares have been falling

item.title: JPMorgan banker fights back over ‘fabricated’ sex harassment claims
item.description: Female leveraged finance executive says allegations are concocted for ‘improper purpose of personal enrichment’

item.title: OpenAI readies IPO filing to list as soon as September
item.description: AI lab is preparing for $1tn listing with bankers Morgan Stanley and Goldman Sachs and lawyers at Cooley

item.title: AI’s adoption problem
item.description: Also in this newsletter, ‘cycle syncing’ your workflow and the joys of small talk

item.title: Blackstone’s $5bn data centre plan melds creativity and necessity
item.description: The idea makes sense, and the opportunity is large

item.title: Germany to overhaul sprawling arms procurement agency
item.description: Move aims to speed up spending of country’s expanded defence budget

item.title: Barney Frank, US congressman, 1940-2026
item.description: First openly gay member of Congress led landmark banking reforms in wake of the financial crisis

item.title: Starmer postpones fuel duty increase
item.description: Planned rise put back until end of year

item.title: Celebrate your looming obsolescence with our new ‘lower-value human capital’ merch
item.description: Because you’re not worth it

item.title: Judge points to son’s money ‘obsession’ in Mango founder death case
item.description: Son of fashion billionaire Isak Andic denies wrongdoing over father’s 100-metre fall on hiking trip

item.title: Italy’s biggest bank explores bid for Spanish wealth manager
item.description: Intesa Sanpaolo prepares formal offer as Warburg Pincus seeks sale of Singular Bank

item.title: Airbus open to two-fighter jet solution amid impasse with France’s Dassault
item.description: France and Germany remain locked in talks to salvage €100bn project plagued by power struggles between the two groups

item.title: JLR plans to develop vehicles in US with Stellantis
item.description: Jeep and Peugeot owner also strikes deal with Dongfeng to produce Chinese group’s electric cars in France

item.title: Protests spread in Africa as fuel crisis deepens
item.description: Sharp rises in diesel and petrol prices spark violent clashes in Kenya and Comoros and strikes in Mozambique

item.title: The oil shock is coming for America
item.description: Tools that worked to lower prices in the last crisis have already been spent

item.title: ‘Lazy scapegoating’: supermarket price cap plan prompts furious backlash
item.description: Treasury’s effort to get UK retailers to agree on voluntary price controls struggles to find any support

item.title: Swiss finance faces some awkward questions
item.description: Activists have triggered a vote on becoming a ‘more sustainable financial centre’

item.title: How can we ensure our children inherit equal amounts?
item.description: My wife and I have children from previous marriages and worry about what will happen once one of us dies

item.title: Camping tech to conquer the great outdoors
item.description: Essential kit – from flasks to flashlights

item.title: FirstFT: Trump critic Thomas Massie loses Kentucky primary
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Europe’s largest property deal in years collapses and Apollo’s Marc Rowan accused of improperly using company resources
